message:
fix(fwc): config-import redacted placeholder false-positive on literal "[REDACTED]" values

The previous config_redacted_placeholder_paths walker flagged every
"[REDACTED]" string in the imported JSON, even when the field was not
actually a secret. This caused spurious rejection of configs where a
user intentionally set a value to the literal string "[REDACTED]".

Now the walker accepts a redacted_fields slice from the config snapshot
and only flags "[REDACTED]" strings whose JSON Pointer path appears in
that list. A new encode_json_pointer_segment helper handles RFC 6901
escaping (~0, ~1).

Adds a test (execute_config_import_allows_literal_redacted_strings_
outside_secret_paths) that verifies the fix end-to-end.

Also removes two unused imports in workflow.rs surfaced by the change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
